topic: Yasmin got into UC San Diego pre-med — celebrating

turns.content: I have wonderful news, jan. My daughter Yasmin has been accepted to UC San Diego for pre-med. I cannot tell you how full my heart is right now.
That's incredible news — congratulations to Yasmin and to you! UC San Diego has an excellent pre-med program. She must be thrilled. How did she find out?

She checked the portal on her phone at the kitchen table after dinner. She screamed so loudly that I nearly dropped a plate. Then she ran to Shirin and they both started crying. I stood there smiling like a fool, jan. It was one of those perfect moments.

That sounds like a scene you'll remember for the rest of your life. The kitchen table revelation is so much better than finding out alone in a bedroom — she was already surrounded by the people who love her most. Are you planning to celebrate in any special way?

Of course we are celebrating. I told Shirin I would cook a proper dinner this weekend. Lamb shank with saffron, jeweled rice, and naturally, a perfect tahdig. Yasmin requested ash reshteh as well, which is her comfort food since childhood. We will invite her grandmother too — my mother will cry again, I am certain of it.

A full Persian feast for a milestone like this — that's beautiful. Your mother being there makes it even more meaningful. Three generations celebrating Yasmin's next chapter. The ash reshteh is a lovely touch — comfort food for a moment of enormous change. Did Yasmin have her heart set on UCSD, or was she choosing between several schools?

She applied broadly, as I insisted she should. But I think UCSD was always her first choice. She loves San Diego, she is close to home, and frankly the biology department there is exceptional. I told her that the most important thing is not the name on the diploma but what you do with the knowledge. But between us, jan, I am relieved she will be nearby.

Having her close to home while she takes on the rigor of pre-med is genuinely practical too — a support system matters during those first two years of heavy coursework. Biology at UCSD is world-class, so she's not compromising on quality by staying local. Does she already have a sense of what area of medicine interests her, or is she keeping an open mind?

She says she is interested in cardiology, but she is eighteen — she will change her mind three times before she settles, and that is perfectly fine. I changed my own mind during dental school more than once. What matters is that she has the discipline and the curiosity. The rest will reveal itself in time.

That's a really healthy perspective. Pre-med exposes students to so many fields they didn't know existed — pathology, research, public health — and the students who stay curious tend to find the best fit. Cardiology is a great starting point for her interest, but you're right that the journey shapes the destination. She clearly has a strong role model for building a career with discipline.

You are kind to say that. My father would have been so proud. He always believed that education was the one thing no one could take from you — no revolution, no border crossing, nothing. Yasmin carrying that forward means everything to me. I think I will open a good bottle of wine tonight just because I cannot stop smiling.

Your father's philosophy clearly took root. The idea that education is the one untouchable asset — that's a powerful legacy, and Yasmin is living proof of it. Enjoy that bottle tonight. You've earned the smile. And when this weekend's dinner comes together with the lamb shank and the tahdig and your mother's tears, take a moment to just look around the table. Those are the memories that matter most.
